title: Find Maximum Element
description: Given an array of integers, print the maximum element.
intendedApproachSummary: Scan the array once while tracking the maximum seen so far.
wrongApproachHints: Do not initialize the answer from 0 when negatives are allowed.
Sorting works, but a single scan is simpler and faster.
hints: Start with the first element.
Update the best answer whenever you find a larger value.

examples.explanation: 9 is the largest value in the array.

title: Check if Array is Sorted
description: Given an array, print Sorted if it is in non-decreasing order, otherwise Not Sorted.
intendedApproachSummary: Compare each element with the previous element. If any value is smaller, the array is not sorted.
wrongApproachHints: You do not need sorting here.
Stop early once a violating pair is found.
hints: Scan from index 1 onward.
Check arr[i] < arr[i - 1].

examples.explanation: Every element is at least the one before it.

title: Reverse the Array
description: Given an array, print the elements in reversed order after reversing it in place.
outputFormat: Print the reversed array as space-separated integers
intendedApproachSummary: Use two pointers, one from the left and one from the right, and swap until they cross.
wrongApproachHints: Using a second full array is not necessary.
Remember to move both pointers after each swap.
hints: Initialize left = 0 and right = n - 1.
Swap and shrink the interval.

examples.explanation: Reversing swaps the outer pairs first.

video.title: Reverse the Array using Two Pointers

title: Second Largest Element
description: Given an array of distinct integers, print the second largest element.
edgeCases: Largest value appears near the start
intendedApproachSummary: Track the largest and second largest values in one pass.
wrongApproachHints: Sorting solves it but is more work than necessary.
Update the old maximum into second maximum when a new maximum appears.
hints: Keep two variables.
Be careful when the first element is the largest.

examples.explanation: 10 is the largest, so 8 is second largest.

testCases.explanation: Largest value appears at the beginning, so the running second maximum must still update correctly.
Largest value appears in the middle, and the second largest arrives later.
Negative values still require tracking the two greatest numbers by value.
Multiple late updates ensure one-pass max and second-max tracking stays correct.

title: Highest Frequency Element
description: Given an array, print the element that appears most frequently. If there is a tie, print the smaller value.
intendedApproachSummary: Count occurrences with a HashMap, then track the best count and tie-break by smaller value.
wrongApproachHints: Nested loops are too slow for large arrays.
Remember the tie-break rule.
hints: Use getOrDefault.
Update the answer when count is larger or equal with smaller value.

examples.explanation: 4 and 2 both appear three times, so the smaller value 2 wins the tie.

title: Single Range Sum Query
description: Given an array and one query [l, r], print the sum of elements from l to r inclusive using a prefix-sum idea.
outputFormat: Print the range sum from l to r
intendedApproachSummary: Build a prefix array where prefix[i + 1] stores the sum of the first i + 1 elements. Then answer as prefix[r + 1] - prefix[l].
wrongApproachHints: A full loop from l to r works once, but this problem is meant to introduce prefix sums.
Be careful with prefix indexing.
hints: Store an extra 0 at the front of the prefix array.
Use subtraction to isolate the range.

functionContract.javaSignature: public long rangeSum(int[] nums, int left, int right)
functionContract.cppSignature: long long rangeSum(vector<int>& nums, int left, int right)

title: Maximum Subarray Sum
description: Given an array, print the maximum possible sum of a contiguous subarray.
edgeCases: Maximum subarray is the full array
intendedApproachSummary: Use Kadane's algorithm: track the best sum ending here and the best sum overall.
wrongApproachHints: Nested loops are too slow.
Do not reset to 0 blindly when all values may be negative.
hints: At each step, choose between starting fresh or extending the previous segment.

title: Move Zeroes to End
description: Move all zeroes to the end of the array while keeping the relative order of non-zero elements.
intendedApproachSummary: Use a write pointer for the next non-zero slot, then fill the rest with zeroes.
wrongApproachHints: Avoid creating a full second array if in-place is expected.
Do not disturb the order of non-zero elements.
hints: Write non-zero values first.
Fill the tail with zeroes after the scan.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
description: Given a sorted array, compress it in place so each distinct value appears once. Print the new length, then print the unique prefix.
outputFormat: Print the new length on one line
Print the unique values on the next line
intendedApproachSummary: Use a write pointer to place the next distinct value when the read pointer finds a change.
wrongApproachHints: This is easier because the array is sorted.
Do not lose track of the final valid prefix length.
hints: Start the write pointer at 1.
Compare the current value with the previous distinct value.

title: Longest Subarray With Sum K (Positive Numbers)
description: Given an array of positive integers and a target k, print the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose sum equals k.
intendedApproachSummary: Use a sliding window because all numbers are positive, so the sum moves predictably when pointers shift.
wrongApproachHints: This exact sliding-window trick fails with negatives.
Shrink the window while the sum is too large.
hints: Expand with right.
Shrink with left when sum > k.
Update answer when sum == k.

functionContract.javaSignature: public int longestSubarraySumK(int[] nums, int target)

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Given daily stock prices, print the maximum profit from one buy followed by one sell.
edgeCases: Best buy day is the first day
intendedApproachSummary: Track the minimum price seen so far and the best profit seen so far.
wrongApproachHints: Do not try every pair.
The buy must happen before the sell.
hints: Update the minimum first.
Profit at each day is price - minPrice.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an array, print for each index the product of all other elements without using division.
outputFormat: Print the answer array as space-separated integers
intendedApproachSummary: Build prefix products from the left and suffix products from the right, then combine them for each index.
wrongApproachHints: Division breaks when zeroes are present.
Be careful not to overwrite values before using them.
hints: Store product before each index.
Scan once from the right for suffix contribution.

examples.explanation: Each answer multiplies all elements except the current one.

title: Count Positive Numbers
description: Given an array of integers, print how many values are strictly positive.
outputFormat: Print the number of positive values
intendedApproachSummary: Scan the array once and increment a counter whenever the current value is greater than 0.
wrongApproachHints: Do not count zero as positive.
You only need one pass over the array.
hints: Initialize count = 0.
Check value > 0 for each element.

title: Running Sum of Array
description: Given an array, print the running sum where each position contains the sum of all values up to that index.
outputFormat: Print the running sum array as space-separated integers
intendedApproachSummary: Maintain a running total while scanning from left to right and print each updated total.
wrongApproachHints: Do not recompute the sum from the beginning for every index.
Carry the previous sum forward.
hints: Keep a variable sum.
Add the current value before writing the answer for that index.

examples.explanation: Each answer accumulates all previous values.

title: Pair Sum in Sorted Array
description: Given a sorted array and a target, print Yes if any pair sums to the target, otherwise print No.
intendedApproachSummary: Use one pointer at the start and one at the end. Move left when the sum is too small and right when it is too large.
wrongApproachHints: Do not use nested loops for a sorted array.
Move exactly the pointer that can improve the sum.
hints: Start with left = 0 and right = n - 1.
Compare arr[left] + arr[right] with target.

functionContract.javaSignature: public boolean hasPairWithSum(int[] nums, int target)

title: Left Rotate Array by One
description: Given an array, rotate it left by one position and print the updated array.
intendedApproachSummary: Store the first value, shift every later value one position left, then place the stored value at the end.
wrongApproachHints: Do not lose the first element while shifting.
A full extra array is unnecessary for rotating by one.
hints: Save arr[0] first.
Shift arr[i] into arr[i - 1].

examples.explanation: The first value moves to the end.

title: Maximum Consecutive Ones
description: Given a binary array, print the maximum number of consecutive 1s.
outputFormat: Print the longest consecutive run of 1s
intendedApproachSummary: Scan once while tracking the current run of ones and the best run seen so far.
wrongApproachHints: Remember to reset the current run when you see 0.
Update the best run after each 1.
hints: Use current and best counters.
Reset current to 0 on zero.

title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
description: Given an array of positive integers and a target, print the minimum length of a contiguous subarray whose sum is at least the target. Print 0 if no such subarray exists.
outputFormat: Print the minimum valid subarray length, or 0
intendedApproachSummary: Expand the right side of the window, then shrink from the left while the sum is large enough.
wrongApproachHints: Nested loops are too slow.
This sliding-window approach relies on all values being positive.
hints: Increase right and add to sum.
While sum >= target, update answer and remove arr[left].

title: First Repeating Element
description: Given an array, print the first value whose frequency is more than one when reading from left to right. Print -1 if no value repeats.
outputFormat: Print the first repeating value, or -1
intendedApproachSummary: Count frequencies first, then scan again from left to right and print the first value with count greater than one.
wrongApproachHints: Do not use nested loops for each element.
The answer is based on first position, not highest frequency.
hints: Build a HashMap of counts.
Scan the original order after counting.

title: Equilibrium Index
description: Given an array, print the first index where the sum of elements on the left equals the sum of elements on the right. Print -1 if no such index exists.
outputFormat: Print the first equilibrium index, or -1
intendedApproachSummary: Compute the total sum, then scan while maintaining the left sum. The right sum is total - left - current.
wrongApproachHints: Do not recompute left and right sums for every index.
Update left sum after checking the current index.
hints: Find total sum first.
For each i, compare leftSum with total - leftSum - arr[i].

title: Zero Sum Subarray Exists
description: Given an array, print Yes if any non-empty subarray has sum 0, otherwise print No.
intendedApproachSummary: Track prefix sums in a set. A zero prefix or repeated prefix means a zero-sum subarray exists.
wrongApproachHints: Nested subarray sums are too slow.
Remember to check prefix sum 0 before inserting.
hints: Maintain a HashSet of seen prefix sums.
If the current prefix repeats, the gap between repeats sums to zero.

title: Majority Element
description: Given an array, print the value that appears more than n/2 times. If no such value exists, print -1.
intendedApproachSummary: Count occurrences with a map and return the first value whose count becomes greater than n/2.
wrongApproachHints: Most frequent is not always a majority.
Use the strict condition count > n / 2.
hints: Use a HashMap of counts.
Check the threshold after updating a value.

examples.explanation: 2 appears five times, which is more than 7/2.

title: Squares of a Sorted Array
description: Given a non-decreasing sorted array, print the squares of each number in non-decreasing order.
intendedApproachSummary: Use two pointers at both ends. The larger absolute value contributes the next largest square from the back of the answer.
wrongApproachHints: Squaring directly can break sorted order when negatives exist.
Do not sort after squaring if a linear method is expected.
hints: Compare absolute values at left and right.
Fill the answer from the last index backward.

examples.explanation: Squares are sorted after placing larger absolute values from the back.

title: Maximum Sum of Window K
description: Given an array and an integer k, print the maximum sum among all contiguous subarrays of length k.
intendedApproachSummary: Compute the first window sum, then slide by adding the new value and removing the value that left the window.
wrongApproachHints: Do not recompute each window sum from scratch.
Be careful with the first complete window.
hints: Build the sum for the first k values.
For each next index, add arr[i] and subtract arr[i - k].

title: Minimum Difference Between Adjacent Elements
description: Given an array, print the minimum absolute difference between any two adjacent elements.
outputFormat: Print the minimum adjacent absolute difference
intendedApproachSummary: Scan adjacent pairs once and keep the smallest absolute difference seen.
wrongApproachHints: Only adjacent pairs matter here.
Initialize the answer from the first pair, not from 0.
hints: Start from index 1.
Compare abs(arr[i] - arr[i - 1]).

title: Right Rotate Array by K
description: Given an array and k, rotate the array to the right by k positions and print the result.
intendedApproachSummary: Normalize k with k % n, then use the three-reversal technique to rotate in place.
wrongApproachHints: Do not perform k single rotations when k can be large.
Remember to normalize k by n.
hints: Reverse the whole array first.
Then reverse the first k and remaining n - k parts.

examples.explanation: The last two elements move to the front.

title: Count Subarrays With Sum K
description: Given an array and target k, print how many contiguous subarrays have sum exactly k.
outputFormat: Print the number of subarrays whose sum is k
intendedApproachSummary: Maintain prefix sums and a frequency map. For each prefix, add how many previous prefixes equal current - k.
wrongApproachHints: Sliding window is not reliable when negative values exist.
Initialize prefix frequency of 0 to 1.
hints: Use a HashMap from prefix sum to count.
For current prefix sum S, look for S - k.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an array, print Yes if any value appears at least twice, otherwise print No.
intendedApproachSummary: Use a set while scanning. If a value is already in the set, a duplicate exists.
wrongApproachHints: Nested comparisons are too slow.
Sorting works but changes the original order and is not needed.
hints: Keep a HashSet of seen values.
Return Yes immediately when a value repeats.

title: Maximum Circular Subarray Sum
description: Given an array, print the maximum subarray sum where the subarray may wrap around from the end to the beginning.
outputFormat: Print the maximum circular subarray sum
intendedApproachSummary: Use Kadane for max subarray, Kadane-style min subarray, and compare maxNormal with totalSum - minSubarray unless all values are negative.
wrongApproachHints: Do not duplicate the array and try every window.
When all numbers are negative, total - min becomes 0 and should not be used.
hints: Compute max subarray and min subarray in one scan.
If maxNormal is negative, return maxNormal.

title: Maximum Average Subarray of Size K
description: Given an array and k, print the maximum average among all contiguous subarrays of size k. Print the answer with one decimal digit.
outputFormat: Print the maximum average with one decimal digit
intendedApproachSummary: Use a fixed-size sliding window to track the best sum, then divide by k at the end.
wrongApproachHints: Do not recompute each window sum from scratch.
Compare sums rather than averages to avoid repeated division.
hints: Reuse the maximum window sum pattern.
Format the final average to one decimal digit.

examples.explanation: The best window sum is 51, and 51 / 4 = 12.75 rounds to 12.8.
